tasks.subject: General Tamil (Podhu Tamil)
tasks.short_description: Exhaustive study of Thirukkural's structure, history, and the 19 essential chapters for Group 4.

learning_content.introduction: Thirukkural is the crown jewel of Tamil literature. Composed by the divine poet Thiruvalluvar, it transcends time, religion, and geography. It is the most translated non-religious work in the world after the Bible and the Quran.

content_blocks.text: While little is historically verified about Thiruvalluvar's life, tradition places him in Mylapore, Chennai. He is revered by many names: Deiva Pulavar, Naanmuganaar, Madhaanubangi, and Poyyamozhi Pulavar. The era of the Kural is generally accepted as the 2nd century BCE to 2nd century CE, belonging to the Pathinenkilkanakku collection of the post-Sangam age. His work is so universal that he never mentions 'Tamil', 'Tamil Nadu', or any specific deity, earning it the title 'Ulaga Podhumarai' (The Universal Veda).

image.caption: Traditional depiction of Thiruvalluvar in deep meditation.

pyqs.question: Which collection does Thirukkural belong to?

content_blocks.text: The Kural is uniquely structured into 'Muppal'—three major sections. Aram (Virtue) sets the moral foundation, Porul (Wealth/Statecraft) provides practical guidance for social and political life, and Inbam (Love) explores the psychological depths of human relationships. This structure reflects the Purusharthas of Indian philosophy but remains strictly secular. It contains 133 chapters (Adhikarams), each containing exactly 10 couplets.

list_items: Total Couplets: 1,330 Kurals.
Total Chapters: 133 Adhikarams (10 Kurals each).
Aram (Virtue): 38 Chapters - focusing on domestic and ascetic life.
Porul (Wealth/Politics): 70 Chapters - the largest section, dealing with kings and society.
Inbam (Love): 25 Chapters - focusing on the inner life (Agam).
Words: Exactly 7 words per Kural (4 in the first line, 3 in the second).

content_blocks.text: The Kural's wisdom has crossed all borders. The Latin translation by Veeramamunivar (Beschi) was the first major European translation. G.U. Pope's English translation brought it to the global stage. It has also been translated into German, French, and Russian. Many world leaders, including Mahatma Gandhi and Leo Tolstoy, have praised the Kural for its simplicity and profound truth.

list_items: Anbuudaimai: Defines love as the life-giving force.
Ozhukkamudaimai: States that discipline is more precious than life.
Panbudaimai: Importance of good social qualities.
Iniyavai Koorald: The merit of speaking pleasant words.

questions.question: Who translated Kural into English?
questions.explanation: G.U. Pope translated it into English.

questions.question: Number of words in the second line of a Kural?
The word 'Tamil' appears how many times in Kural?
One Kural has how many words?

tasks.subject: General Science
tasks.short_description: Nature of Universe, General scientific laws, and Force/Motion.

learning_content.introduction: The study of Physics begins with understanding the vast Universe and the fundamental laws that govern force and motion.

content_blocks.text: The Universe consists of billions of galaxies, each containing billions of stars. The Big Bang theory is the leading explanation for its origin about 13.8 billion years ago. Our Solar System is located in the Orion Arm of the Milky Way galaxy.

tasks.subject: Aptitude & Mental Ability
tasks.short_description: Core mental ability concepts for speed and accuracy.

learning_content.introduction: Simplification and Number systems are the foundation of all aptitude problems in TNPSC exams.

list_items: Product of two numbers = HCF × LCM.
HCF is the highest common factor.
LCM is the least common multiple.
Use Prime Factorization method for large numbers.

tasks.subject: General Tamil (Podhu Tamil)
tasks.short_description: Deep analysis of the secondary Sangam collection focusing on Purananuru.

learning_content.introduction: Ettuthogai represents the sociological records of the ancient Tamils, divided into Agam (Inner/Love) and Puram (Outer/War).

content_blocks.text: Purananuru, consisting of 400 songs by various poets, is the 'Encyclopedia of Tamil Culture'. It captures the heroism of the Muvendar, the generosity of the Seven Great Patrons (Kadaiyezhu Vallalgal), and the philosophical outlook of the common people. It famously contains the verse 'Yaadhum Oore Yaavarum Kelir' (Every place is my home, every human is my kin) by Kaniyan Pungundranar. The poems were mostly composed by assembly poets known as 'Panars' and 'Viraliars'.

image.caption: Replica of ancient palm leaf manuscripts.

list_items: Natrinai: 400 poems on love, 9-12 lines each.
Kurunthogai: 401 poems, 4-8 lines each (The most edited work).
Aingurunuru: 500 poems (Shortest, 3-6 lines).
Pathitrupathu: 80 available poems, exclusively on Chera Kings.
Paripadal: Musical poems on Gods like Murugan and rivers like Vaigai.
Kalithogai: set in Kali meter with dramatic dialogue.
Agananuru: 400 long poems (13-31 lines), divided into 3 parts.

pyqs.question: Which work is exclusively on Chera kings?

questions.question: Which work is a mixed category (Agam/Puram)?

tasks.subject: Indian Polity
tasks.short_description: Detailed study of Articles 12-35 (Part III) of the Constitution.

learning_content.introduction: Fundamental Rights are the heart and soul of the Indian Constitution, ensuring liberty and equality.

list_items: Right to Equality (Articles 14-18)
Right to Freedom (Articles 19-22)
Right against Exploitation (Articles 23-24)
Right to Constitutional Remedies (Article 32)

tasks.subject: Geography
tasks.short_description: Southwest and Northeast Monsoons in India and Tamil Nadu.

learning_content.introduction: Climate is the long-term weather pattern of a region.

content_blocks.text: India primarily receives rainfall from the Southwest Monsoon (June-Sept). Tamil Nadu, however, is a rain-shadow region for SW monsoon and receives its major rainfall from the Northeast Monsoon (Oct-Dec).

tasks.subject: History & Culture
tasks.short_description: Founding of Vijayanagara and the reign of Krishnadevaraya.

learning_content.introduction: The Vijayanagara Empire (1336-1646) was founded by Harihara and Bukka.

content_blocks.text: Krishnadevaraya was the greatest ruler. He wrote 'Amuktamalyada' and built the Vitthala temple. His court was graced by the 'Ashtadiggajas'.

learning_content.introduction: Economic planning in India was initially guided by Five Year Plans.

sections.title: Planning Era
list_items: 1st Plan: Focus on Agriculture (1951-56).
2nd Plan: Focus on Heavy Industry (Mahalanobis Model).
NITI Aayog replaced Planning Commission in 2015.

tasks.subject: Current Affairs

learning_content.introduction: Tamil Nadu is a leader in social welfare schemes in India.

sections.title: Recent Initiatives
